title: Senior Software Engineer - Backend

tailored_resume.name: Sai Manikanta Karnati
tailored_resume.summary: Senior Software Engineer with 5+ years building scalable backend services, distributed data platforms, and cloud-native infrastructure across AWS and Azure. Skilled in Java, Scala, Spark, SQL, Kubernetes, and Databricks, with strong UI/UX delivery. Engineered event-driven systems at 99.99% reliability and cut production issue resolution time by 40% while solving complex real-world data problems.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Architected scalable backend services and platform APIs using Java Spring Boot microservices and Python, enabling independent deployments across 10+ teams.
Engineered Apache Spark and Scala data pipelines on Databricks to process millions of records daily, improving throughput and reducing manual effort ~60%.
Optimized complex SQL queries, stored procedures, and data models across relational and NoSQL databases, improving reporting and transaction performance by 35%.
Built high-throughput event-driven systems using DynamoDB Streams, Python/Node.js Lambda, and SNS/SQS, ensuring 99.99% reliability.
Deployed cloud-native applications on AWS (Lambda, ECS, EC2, S3, RDS) and Azure, orchestrating containers with Kubernetes to scale infrastructure on demand.
Delivered responsive Angular SPAs with strong UI/UX (style color, RGB theming, accessible components), integrating REST and GraphQL APIs for seamless frontend-backend flow.
Implemented monitoring and observability with Splunk and Spring Boot Actuator, reducing production issue resolution time by 40%.

experience.title: Associate Software Engineer
bullets: Developed scalable backend services and RESTful APIs using Java Spring Boot, Python, and Node.js, powering core platform features for enterprise teams.
Built Apache Spark and Scala batch jobs for data transformation and validation, cutting data processing time ~50% across backend workflows.
Optimized SQL queries, joins, views, and stored procedures, improving API response times by 35%.
Designed dynamic Angular UI/UX with TypeScript, SCSS, and reusable components, applying style color and RGB design tokens for consistent interfaces.
Implemented event-driven architecture with Apache Kafka, reducing inter-service dependency failures by 35%.
Automated infrastructure provisioning with Terraform on AWS and GCP, and containerized services with Docker and Kubernetes to streamline CI/CD.
Secured services with OAuth2, JWT, and Spring Security, and tuned Redis caching with SQL optimization to sustain throughput under heavy load.

experience.title: Engineer Trainee
bullets: Developed enterprise applications using Angular frontends and Python/Java backend services to solve real-world business problems.
Built and optimized SQL queries, stored procedures, and relational schemas, improving query execution time by 20% on MySQL and MongoDB.
Prototyped data processing logic in C++ and Java, strengthening performance-critical backend modules.
Developed RESTful APIs documented with Swagger/OpenAPI and analyzed datasets in Excel to validate reporting accuracy.
Built Angular UI/UX modules with TypeScript, Redux, and Reactive Forms for responsive workflows.
Increased unit test coverage to 95% using JUnit and Python-based test automation tools.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.institution: University of North Carolina at Charlotte

certifications: AWS Academy Graduate – AWS Academy Data Engineering

changes: Reconstructed fragmented job entries into three clean roles (Vanguard, Xebia, Xebia) with correct titles and dates parsed from the raw input.
Added a Senior Software Engineer summary mirroring the exact target title with years of experience and a quantified outcome.
Wove required skills (Spark, Scala, Kubernetes, Java, SQL, AWS, Azure, S3, C++, Excel, UI/UX, Databricks) directly into experience bullets rather than leaving them in the Skills list.
Incorporated domain keywords: platform, backend, services, infrastructure, data, teams, solve, real-world problems, style color, RGB.
Rewrote all bullets with strong action verbs (Architected, Engineered, Optimized, Deployed) and removed weak openers.
Merged the UNC Charlotte and MS Computer Science fragments into one education entry; cleaned certification formatting.
ESTIMATED METRICS to confirm: Spark/Databricks pipeline volume (~millions records/day), data processing reductions (~50-60%), and the '10+ teams' scope — please verify these are accurate.
Could not plausibly map the niche tokens 'target blank', 'blank data-saferedirecturl', 'source gmail', 'ust', 'usg', 'benefits', 'status' to real work, so they were omitted to stay truthful.
Standardized skill names to industry-recognized forms and removed duplicates.
